title: Intelligent shading system with movable base assembly
abstract: A shading system includes a base assembly, core assembly, a sensor expansion module coupled to the core assembly module, wherein the sensor expansion module includes one or more arm support assemblies; one or more arms connected to the one or more arm support assemblies. The base assembly includes one or more motors, one or more processors; one or more memory modules; a base motor controller; and computer-readable instructions, which when executed communicate movement direction and distance instructions to a base motor controller. The base assembly includes one or more motors, wherein the base motor controller converts the received movement direction and/or distance instructions to movement direction and/or distance commands, and communicates the movement direction and/or distance commands to the one or more motors to physically move the base assembly.
claims:
Claims IN THE CLAIMS 1 . A base assembly, comprising: a central support connector attached to a central support assembly of an umbrella; and a base assembly, the base assembly comprising: one or more processors; one or more memory modules; a base motor controller; computer-readable instructions, the computer-readable instructions accessed from the one or memory modules and executed by the one or more processors to communicate instructions to the base motor controller; and one or more motors communicatively coupled to the base motor controller, wherein the one or more motors receive movement commands from the base motor controller related to movement of the base assembly. 2. The base assembly of claim 1 , further comprising: a drive assembly, mechanically coupled to the one or more motors, to generate physical movement of the base assembly. 3. The base assembly of claim 2, wherein the drive assembly comprises one or more shafts and one or more wheels, and wherein the one or more motors cause rotation of the one or more shafts and the one or more shafts cause rotation of the one or more wheels to physical move the base assembly a specified distance and/or direction. 4. The base assembly of claim 2, wherein the drive assembly comprises a conveyer system and one or more treads, and and wherein the one or more motors cause rotation of the conveyer system and the conveyer system causes rotation of the one or more treads to physically move the base assembly a specified distance and/or direction. 5. The base assembly of claim 2, wherein the computer-readable instructions comprise pre-programmed instructions with specified movement direction values and distance instructions, - 1 - 

 wherein the computer-readable instructions being executed by the one or more processors to generate movement instructions corresponding to the movement direction values and distance values and communicate the movement instructions to the base assembly motor controller. 6. The base assembly of claim 2, further comprising one or more environmental sensors, the one or more environmental sensors to capture measurements of environmental conditions, communicate the captured measurements to the one or more processors, wherein the computer-readable instructions, when executed by the one or more processors, analyze the captured measurements of the environmental conditions, generate direction and distance movement instructions and communicate the generated direction and distance movement instructions to the base motor controller. 7. The base assembly of claim 2, further comprising one or more directional sensors, the one or more directional sensors to calculate measurements related to location of the shading system and communicate the captured measurements related to location of the shading system to the one or more processors, wherein the computer-readable instructions, when executed by the one or more processors, analyze the captured measurements related to location of the shading system, generate direction and distance movement instructions and communicate the generated direction and distance movement instructions to the base motor controller. 8. The base assembly of claim 2, further comprising one or more proximity sensors, the one or more proximity sensors to generate an indicator that an object is present near the base assembly and to calculate a location of the object, and communicate the generated indicator and the calculated location of the object to the one or more processors, wherein the computer-readable instructions, when executed by the one or more processors, analyze the captured location of the object, generate direction and distance movement instructions, based at least in part on the captured location, and - 2 - 

 communicate the generated direction and distance movement instructions to the base motor controller. 9. The base assembly of claim 2, further comprising a wireless transceiver, the wireless transceiver to receive generated direction and distance movement instructions from an external computing device and to communicate the received direction and distance movement instructions to the base motor controller. 10. A shading system, comprising: a core assembly; a sensor expansion module coupled to the core assembly module, wherein the sensor expansion module comprises: one or more arm support assemblies; one or more arms connected to the one or more arm support assemblies; a shading fabric attached at least one of the one or more arms; and one or more solar panel assemblies to convert solar energy into electrical energy; and a base assembly, the base assembly coupled to the core assembly via a core assembly connector, the base assembly comprising: one or more processors; one or more memory modules; a base motor controller; and computer-readable instructions, the computer-readable instructions accessed from the one or memory modules and executed by the one or more processors to communicate movement direction and distance instructions to a base motor controller; one or more motors communicatively coupled to the base motor controller, wherein the base motor controller converts the received movement direction and/or distance instructions to movement direction and/or distance commands, and communicates the movement direction and/or distance commands to the one or more motors to physically move the base assembly, based at least in part, on the received movement direction and distance commands. - 3 - 1 1 . The shading system of claim 10, further comprising one or more sensors, wherein one of the one or more sensors is a directional sensor which generates location measurements of the shading system, and the computer-readable instructions being executed by the processor further to analyze solar measurements from the one or more solar panel assemblies and the location measurements from the directional sensor to calculate a light source heading and to generate the movement direction and/or distance instructions based, at least in part on the solar measurements and the light source heading. 12. The shading system of claim 10, further comprising one or more sensors, wherein the one or more sensors are environmental sensors, the environmental sensors capturing measurements of environmental conditions in an area around the shading system and communicating the captured measurements of environmental conditions to the base assembly, and wherein the computer-readable instructions executed by the processor further to analyze the captured measurements of the environmental conditions, generate the movement direction and distance instructions; and communicate the generated movement direction and/or distance instructions to the base motor controller. 13. The shading system of claim 10, further comprising one or more sensors, wherein the one or more sensors are proximity sensors, the proximity sensors generating an indicator that an object is within an area of the shading system and calculating a location of the object with respect to the base assembly, and communicating the generated indicator and the calculated location of the object, and wherein the computer-readable instructions executed by the processor further to analyze the calculated location of the object, generate the movement direction and distance instructions, based at least in part, on the calculated location of the object; and communicate the generated movement direction and/or distance instructions to the base motor controller. 14. The shading system of claim 10, wherein the core assembly further comprises one or more additional processors; one or more additional memory modules; and additional computer- readable instructions stored in the one or more additional memory modules; - 4 - 

 the additional computer-implementable instructions accessed from the one or more additional memory modules and executed by the one or more processors to perform voice recognition on audio files or spoken commands received by the shading system, generate the movement direction and distance instructions, based at least in part, on the audio files or spoken commands, and communicate the generated movement direction and/or distance instructions to the base motor controller. 15. The shading system of claim 10, wherein the core assembly module further comprises one or more additional processors; one or more additional memory modules; and additional computer- readable instructions stored in the one or more additional memory modules; the additional computer-implementable instructions accessed from the one or more additional memory modules and executed by the one or more processors to communicate audio files or spoken commands received by the shading system to a remote server for voice recognition, to receive from the remote server converted voice commands based, at least in part, on audio files or spoken commands, to generate the movement direction and distance instructions based at least in part on the converted voice commands, and communicate the generated movement direction and/or distance instructions to the base motor controller. 16. The shading system of claim 10, further comprising one or more sensors, wherein the one or more sensors are environmental sensors, the environmental sensors capturing measurements of environmental conditions in area around the shading system and communicating the captured measurements of environmental conditions; wherein the core assembly further comprises one or more additional processors; one or more additional memory modules; and additional computer- readable instructions stored in the one or more additional memory modules; and wherein the additional computer-readable instructions executed by the processor further to analyze the captured measurements of the environmental conditions, generate the movement direction and distance instructions; and communicate the generated movement direction and/or distance instructions to the base motor controller in the base assembly. - 5 - 17. The shading system of claim 10, further comprising one or more sensors, wherein the one or more sensors are proximity sensors, the proximity sensors generating an indicator an object is within an area of the shading system and calculating a location of the object and communicating the generated indicator and the calculated location of the object with respect to the shading system; wherein the core assembly further comprises one or more additional processors; one or more additional memory modules; and additional computer- readable instructions stored in the one or more additional memory modules; wherein the additional computer-readable instructions executed by the one or more additional processors further to analyze the calculated location of the object, generate the movement direction and distance instructions based at least in part on the calculated location of the object; and communicate the generated movement direction and/or distance instructions to the base motor controller 18. The base assembly of claim 1 , further comprising a camera to capture video of an area around the base assembly, wherein the computer-readable instructions executed by the one or more processors further to analyze the captured video to identify objects, to generate the movement direction and distance instructions based on the identified object, and to communicate the generated movement direction and/or distance instructions to the base motor controller. 19. The shading system of claim 10, further comprising a camera to capture video of an area around the base assembly, wherein the core assembly further comprises one or more additional processors; one or more additional memory modules; and additional computer- readable instructions stored in the one or more additional memory modules; wherein the additional computer-readable instructions executed by the additional one or more the processors further to analyze the captured video to identify objects, to calculate locations of the objects; to generate the movement direction and/or distance instructions based at least in part on the calculated locations of the objects; and to communicate the generated movement direction and/or distance instructions to the base motor controller in the base assembly. - 6 -
